hints.dico only: There are two verbs: 'dico' (I say) and 'surge' (rise!, imperative)
hints.surge only: 'surge' is an imperative verb, but 'dico' (I say) is also a finite verb
hints.Iuvenis and dico: 'Iuvenis' is a noun (young man), not a verb

hints.perfect: Perfect 1st person would be 'dixi' — 'dico' is present
hints.future: Future would be 'dicam' — 'dico' is present indicative

hints.duco, ducere, duxi, ductum: These are principal parts of 'duco' (to lead)
hints.do, dare, dedi, datum: These are principal parts of 'do' (to give)
hints.disco, discere, didici, —: These are principal parts of 'disco' (to learn)

hints.present indicative: Present indicative would be 'surgit' — 'surge' is a command
hints.subjunctive: Subjunctive would be 'surgat' — 'surge' is the imperative singular
hints.infinitive: Infinitive would be 'surgere' — 'surge' is the imperative

hints.servo, servare, servavi, servatum: These are principal parts of 'servo' (to save/guard)
hints.rego, regere, rexi, rectum: These are principal parts of 'rego' (to rule)
hints.pello, pellere, pepuli, pulsum: These are principal parts of 'pello' (to drive)

hints.nominative: 'Iuvenis' is nominative in form, but here Jesus addresses the young man directly — it is vocative
hints.accusative: Accusative would be 'iuvenem' — 'Iuvenis' here is vocative (direct address)
hints.genitive: Genitive would be 'iuvenis' (same form) but context shows this is vocative

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'iuvenis' has genitive in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns have genitive in -i — 'iuvenis' has genitive in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -us — 'iuvenis' has genitive in -is

hints.accusative: Accusative 2nd person is 'te' — 'tibi' is dative (I say TO you)

translation: Young man, I say to you, arise
